label: PI-122 build-closure — Agent Profile Registry (ADO §10 follow-on)

session.session_executive_summary: PI-122 (Agent Profile Registry, the ADO §10 follow-on, PRJ-018) built + closed on pi-122-registry, merged FF to main. A REST/access/MCP substrate on the landed PI-123 unified DB + PI-γ principals: four system/shared entities with a nullable engagement_id scope (NULL=system, set=overlay) — agent_profile, skill, governance_rule, learning. A resolver composes a profile into an effective contract (prompt + tools + enforced ruleset + active learnings + version stamp) via the System ∪ overlay merge; a write-back lifecycle captures -> accumulates -> promotes (enforced rules always human-reviewed) -> curates, with cross-engagement promotion (DEC-373). MCP tools + two proven prompts seeded; agent_profile=template, PI-γ principal=instance. Records DEC-380, resolves PI-122.
session.session_description: PI-122 build-closure: ingests the seven commits (Architecture pass + six Development slices), records the build as DEC-380, and resolves PI-122. Built scope-aware against the unified DB directly (its blocked_by PI-123 is Resolved). Merged FF to main at 6fe3a98.

conversation.conversation_purpose: Close out PI-122: ingest the build commits, record the build decision, and resolve PI-122.
conversation.conversation_description: Built the Agent Profile Registry substrate (4 entities, resolver, write-back lifecycle, cross-engagement learning, MCP tools, seed) across an Architecture pass + six slices and authored this build-closure.
conversation.conversation_summary: Resolved PI-122; recorded DEC-380; ingested the seven PI-122 commits.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-122 Architecture/scoping pass — Agent Profile Registry
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-122 Architecture/scoping pass — Agent Profile Registry

Build-ready design for PI-122 (the ADO §10 agent-profile registry), turning
registry PRD v0.3 into a plan against the now-landed foundation (PI-123 unified
DB + PI-beta header-per-request + PI-gamma principals). Key decisions:

- D-δ1: four governance entities — agent_profile (AGP-), skill (SKL-),
  governance_rule (GVR-), learning (LRN-).
- D-δ2 (load-bearing): scope-awareness = a NULLABLE engagement_id on plain Base
  tables (NULL=system, set=engagement overlay), mirroring PI-gamma's
  role_assignments — NOT EngagementScopedMixin (which would force NOT NULL +
  hide system rows). Cross-engagement learning becomes a GROUP BY across
  engagement_id, the unified-DB payoff the PRD predicted.
- D-δ3: roster per (area × tier); seed the two proven prompts as system profiles.
- D-δ4: resolver returns a runtime-ready effective contract = System ∪
  engagement-overlay (+ active learnings).
- D-δ5: agent_profile=template, PI-gamma service_agent principal=instance; the
  runtime joins them via mint_agent_principal(agent_tier/agent_area) — the
  columns PI-gamma added anticipating this. No PI-gamma change needed.
- D-δ6: learning_derived_from→finding edge lands with the reconciliation build;
  PI-122 ships learning + edges to today's targets, decoupled.
- D-δ7: promotion gate reuses hybrid governance (enforced rules always human).

6-slice build order + maps onto the existing WSK-002..007 phase workstreams.
Runtime scheduler stays the separate §10 follow-on. No build code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-122 slice 1 — Agent Profile Registry catalog entities (scope-aware)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-122 slice 1 — Agent Profile Registry catalog entities (scope-aware)

Three catalog governance entities (D-δ1) + the learning table (built in slice 3):
- models: AgentProfileRow (AGP-), SkillRow (SKL-), GovernanceRuleRow (GVR-),
  LearningRow (LRN-) — plain Base with a NULLABLE engagement_id (D-δ2: NULL=system,
  set=engagement overlay), identifier-format + vocab + status CHECKs.
- vocab: 4 entity types + AGENT_PROFILE_TIERS / SKILL_KINDS / RULE_ENFORCEMENT_MODES
  / REGISTRY_STATUSES / LEARNING_* enums.
- access: agent_profiles / skills / governance_rules repos (CRUD + change_log +
  server-assigned identifiers) + shared _registry scope helper (scope<->engagement_id).
- api: registry router (agent-profiles / skills / governance-rules) + Create/Update
  schemas; entity_summary specs for the 4 new types (coverage guard).
- migrations: SQLite 0043 + PG 0005 — create the 4 tables (from ORM __table__) +
  rebuild change_log/refs entity-type CHECKs from current vocab (the add-ENTITY_TYPE
  gotcha), guarded for the create_all-then-upgrade-head path.
- tests: access (7) + API (4) + migration round-trip (2).

Full SQLite suite green (2228 passed; only the documented pre-existing 18 fail:
test_0037 + 17 apply_close_out branch-guard off main). MCP tools, bindings,
resolver, learning lifecycle, panels: later slices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-122 slice 2 — binding edges + contract resolver (D-δ4)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-122 slice 2 — binding edges + contract resolver (D-δ4)

- vocab: 5 registry edge kinds in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS (agent_profile_has_skill,
  agent_profile_governed_by_rule + the 3 learning edges, admitted now so the
  learning slice needs no further CHECK migration); _kinds_for_pair clauses for
  the 2 binding pairs (learning clauses land with the learning repo).
- access/repositories/registry_resolver.py: resolve_contract(profile_id, engagement_id)
  composes the effective contract — system_prompt (description + instruction skills +
  advisory rules), tools (tool-skill io_contracts), enforced_ruleset, version_stamp
  (deterministic hash; changes when the profile or any bound item changes), and the
  scope merge (system rows ∪ active-engagement overlay; active_learnings empty until
  slice 4).
- api: GET /agent-profiles/{id}/contract (engagement from ?engagement or the
  X-Engagement header).
- migrations: SQLite 0044 + PG 0006 rebuild the refs relationship_kind CHECK.
- tests: binding + resolution + scope-merge + version-stamp change (3).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-122 slice 3 — learning entity + capture/evidence + resolver injection
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-122 slice 3 — learning entity + capture/evidence + resolver injection

- vocab: _kinds_for_pair clauses for the learning edges (learning_derived_from →
  work_task/decision/test_spec; learning_contradicted_by → work_task;
  learning_promoted_to → skill/governance_rule). finding target deferred (D-δ6).
- access/repositories/learnings.py: CRUD + capture (at Work-Task close, optional
  evidence edge, confidence 1) + add_evidence (supporting raises confidence,
  contradicting lowers it — the curation trigger; PRD §13.2).
- registry_resolver: inject active (area, tier) learnings in scope into the
  contract (matched to the profile's area+tier; orchestrator/pi_lead profiles get
  none; all-active today, relevance retrieval is the scale follow-on).
- api: learnings router (CRUD + /capture + /{id}/evidence) + schemas.
- tests: capture/hunch, accumulate/contradict, resolver injection + area/tier
  scoping (6).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-122 slice 4 — write-back lifecycle (promote + curate, D-δ7)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-122 slice 4 — write-back lifecycle (promote + curate, D-δ7)

access/repositories/registry_lifecycle.py + endpoints:
- promote_to_skill / promote_to_rule: link the learning via learning_promoted_to,
  flip it to status=promoted, and inherit its scope. Promoting to an ENFORCED
  rule requires human_approved=True (the permanent Needs-Attention hard line —
  an agent never self-grants a blocking constraint); advisory + skill promotion
  is free.
- curate_area: the per-(release, area) sweep mechanism — flips contradicted,
  zero-confidence active learnings to stale (the per-release cadence/Release
  entity is the runtime follow-on).
- tests: promote-to-skill linkage, advisory-free, enforced-gate (blocked then
  human-approved), curate retires-vs-survives (4).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-122 slice 5 — cross-engagement learning (DEC-373 multiplier)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-122 slice 5 — cross-engagement learning (DEC-373 multiplier)

registry_lifecycle:
- cross_engagement_candidates: groups active engagement-scoped learnings by
  (area, tier, content) and surfaces those recurring across >= N engagements —
  evidence a learning is universal. The cheap GROUP-BY-content-across-engagement_id
  the unified DB enables (PRD §13.3/§13.5).
- promote_to_system: flips an engagement-scoped learning to system scope so every
  engagement inherits it (a system enforced rule is then the top gate via
  promote_to_rule + human review).
- endpoints: GET /learnings/cross-engagement-candidates, POST
  /learnings/{id}/promote-to-system.
- tests: candidate detection across two engagements + promotion + single-engagement
  exclusion (2).

Scope override/disable of a system rule by an engagement overlay is the remaining
resolver-merge piece (deferred follow-on).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-122 slice 6 — MCP tools + system-profile seed + CLAUDE.md note
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-122 slice 6 — MCP tools + system-profile seed + CLAUDE.md note

- mcp_server/tools.py: registry tools — resolve_agent_profile_contract (an agent
  boots its contract), list/create_agent_profile, list_skills, list_governance_rules,
  list_learnings, capture_learning (agents write learnings back).
- access/repositories/registry_seed.py: seed_system_profiles — the two proven ADO
  prompts (development-phase-specialist -> storage Architect; area-specialist ->
  storage Developer) as system-scoped agent_profiles, idempotent.
- CLAUDE.md: PI-122 Agent Profile Registry substrate note (4 entities, scope model,
  resolver, write-back lifecycle, cross-engagement, template<->principal seam,
  migrations, deferred follow-ons).
- tests: MCP tools registered + create/resolve/capture over MCP + idempotent seed (4).

Full SQLite suite green (2245 passed; only the documented pre-existing 18 fail).
Deferred follow-ons: read-only Qt monitoring panels for the 4 entities; resolver
override/disable of a system rule by an engagement overlay; the runtime scheduler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: PI-122 Agent Profile Registry — build decisions and findings
decisions.context: PI-122 (the ADO §10 agent-profile registry, PRJ-018) was unblocked once its blocked_by PI-123 resolved; PI-α/β/γ further provided the unified DB + principal foundation. The registry PRD v0.3 + the Architecture pass (pi-122-agent-profile-registry-architecture.md) define the build against that landed foundation.
decisions.decision: (1) Four governance entities — agent_profile (AGP-, keyed to an (area × tier) cell), skill (SKL-, instruction|tool + io_contract), governance_rule (GVR-, hybrid advisory|enforced|enforced_with_override), learning (LRN-, evidence-tagged + confidence). (2) Scope-awareness = a NULLABLE engagement_id on plain Base tables (NULL=system, set=engagement overlay) — NOT EngagementScopedMixin (which would force NOT NULL + hide system rows); the resolver does the scope merge explicitly. Mirrors PI-γ's role_assignments. (3) Binding/learning edges in the reference vocab (agent_profile_has_skill, agent_profile_governed_by_rule, learning_derived_from/_contradicted_by/_promoted_to); the learning→finding target waits on the finding entity (an ADO-reconciliation concern). (4) resolve_contract composes prompt + tools + enforced_ruleset + active (area,tier) learnings + a deterministic version_stamp, scope-merged System ∪ active-engagement overlay. (5) Write-back lifecycle: capture-at-Work-Task-close -> accumulate (evidence raises / contradiction lowers confidence) -> promote (to skill = float/pin; to an enforced rule = human review ALWAYS, the Needs-Attention hard line) -> per-(release,area) curate sweep; cross-engagement promotion (engagement->system) is the DEC-373 multiplier. (6) MCP tools (resolve-contract/list/create/capture) + the two proven prompts seeded as system profiles. (7) agent_profile=template, PI-γ service_agent principal (agent_tier/agent_area)=runtime instance.
decisions.rationale: The registry is the ADO §10 follow-on that makes agents thin: they hold an identity and resolve their skills/rules/learnings + a version stamp from the DB. Building it scope-aware against the now-landed unified DB realizes the PRD's prediction — scope is row scope, cross-engagement learning is a GROUP BY across engagement_id — with no special machinery. Reusing the hybrid-governance split for the promotion gate avoids a second safety system; the enforced-rule human-review line is permanent. PI-γ already added agent_tier/agent_area to principals anticipating the template<->instance seam, so no PI-γ rework.
decisions.alternatives_considered: EngagementScopedMixin for the registry tables (rejected: forces engagement_id NOT NULL and applies the read-filter, making system rows impossible / invisible to a scoped query — the explicit resolver merge is correct, consistent with principals/role_assignments). A roles-style separate versioned-catalog table for point-in-time pinning (deferred: pinning stays a drift assertion per PRD §7.4). Pulling the finding entity forward for learning evidence (rejected: it is an ADO-reconciliation build concern — the learning loop runs on work_task/decision/test_spec evidence now and gains finding evidence when that entity lands). Building the Qt panels + runtime scheduler now (deferred: panels are read-only monitoring; the runtime is the separate §10 follow-on).
decisions.consequences: The registry substrate is complete + tested (4 entities + resolver + write-back lifecycle + cross-engagement + MCP + seed). SQLite migrations 0043/0044, PG 0005/0006 (create the 4 tables + rebuild change_log/refs entity-type + relationship_kind CHECKs from current vocab, guarded for the create_all path). Live-DB note: the create_all-managed v2-unified.db got the 4 registry tables added directly (reads work); enabling registry WRITES on the live DB needs the change_log/refs CHECK rebuild (deferred — nothing writes registry records on the live DB until the runtime). Deferred follow-ons: read-only Qt monitoring panels for the 4 entities; the resolver's engagement override/disable of a system rule; and the runtime scheduler (the separate §10 follow-on that resolves a contract + mint_agent_principals the ephemeral agent).
decisions.executive_summary: Records PI-122's build: the Agent Profile Registry as a REST/access/MCP substrate — agent_profile/skill/governance_rule/learning as system/shared rows with a nullable engagement_id scope (the resolver does the System ∪ overlay merge, not EngagementScopedMixin); a contract resolver; a capture->accumulate->promote->curate write-back lifecycle with enforced-rule human review and cross-engagement promotion (DEC-373); MCP tools; and the two proven prompts seeded. agent_profile=template, PI-γ service_agent principal=runtime instance. Deferred: Qt panels, resolver override/disable, the runtime scheduler.
